items.title: Kleine irritatie bij Grim bij onderwerp Tomiyasu: 'Dat is typisch zo'n vraag…'
items.summary: Ajax zit in een schema met 7 wedstrijden in 21 dagen en dus ging het na de gewonnen wedstrijd tegen Villarreal ook veel over de belastbaarheid van spelers en blessuregevallen. Takehiro Tomiyasu passeerde ook de revue tijdens het persmoment.

items.title: NEC ontsnapt weer in beker: 'Wij hebben alles te verliezen'
items.summary: NEC beleefde dinsdag een moeizame avond in het KNVB Bekertoernooi. De Nijmegenaren schakelden buurman De Treffers uit in de achtste finale, maar dat ging niet bepaald met gemak. Uiteindelijk plaatste NEC zich wel voor de kwartfinale, waarin een thuisduel met FC Volendam wacht.

items.title: Lingfield stages eight-race all-weather card
items.summary: Lingfield opens its gates for an eight-race card, live on Sky Sports Racing

items.title: Van profiel tot deal: Stewart en Schaling geven inkijkje in transfer
items.summary: Januari staat in het teken van transfers. Clubs speuren de markt af voor een nieuwe spits, of anders wel een linksback. Maar hoe gaat dat proces eigenlijk in z’n werk? En wat zegt het als een speler in beeld is? Technisch directeur Earnest Stewart en head of recruitment Thomas Schaling van PSV maken dat op verzoek inzichtelijk. ‘Voor elke positie zijn er zes tot twaalf top targets.’

items.title: Raducanu knocked out of Australian Open in second round
items.summary: Emma Raducanu's Australian Open ended in the second round with an error-strewn loss to Anastasia Potapova.

items.title: Bellingham on drinking celeb: 'I know the truth'
items.summary: Jude Bellingham described his drinking gesture goal celebration as "a bit of a joke" aimed at speculation about his off-the-field behaviour.

items.summary: - Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola said "everything is going wrong" for his team after being on the end of a stunning...

items.title: Wrexham rue 'nothing moment' in Leicester draw
items.summary: Wrexham boss Phil Parkinson was left hurting after watching Jannik Vestergaard snatch a point for Leicester in their 1-1 draw but felt there were positives to take from a battling performance in horrible conditions.

items.title: 'Good signs' or just buying time? Spurs remain divided on Frank future
items.summary: Many Tottenham fans have already lost faith in Thomas Frank - but did Tuesday's 2-0 win over Borussia Dortmund give cause for optimism?

items.title: Between Gabriel Jesus and Gyökeres, Arsenal's striker battle is heating up
items.summary: Gabriel Jesus and Viktor Gyökeres each staked their claim to Arsenal's No. 9 spot in their 3-1 win over Inter Milan.

items.title: Vinícius Júnior roars back into form, much to the Bernabéu's delight
items.summary: It's been a rollercoaster of a week, but a convincing win over Monaco has gotten Real Madrid back on track.

items.title: Bellingham responds to partying claims with 'joke' drinking celebration
items.summary: Jude Bellingham responds to partying claims with a goal celebration in the Champions League win over Monaco.

items.title: 'Tears in my eyes' - Jesus enjoys 'dream night' in San Siro
items.summary: Gabriel Jesus says he had "tears in my eyes" after scoring twice to help Arsenal win 3-1 at Inter Milan for a seventh successive win in the Champions League.

items.title: Ajax' kans op bereiken tussenronde Champions League groeit naar 11,5 procent
items.summary: Ajax mag blijven dromen van de tussenronde van de Champions League dankzij Oliver Edvardsen. De aanvaller schiet in de slotfase de Amsterdammers op een 1-2 voorsprong bij Villarreal, dat op voorsprong kwam en Oscar Gloukh de 1-1 zag maken. Lees alles terug in onze VI Live.

items.title: Regeer groeit in leidersrol bij Ajax en gaat in op businessclub-akkefietje
items.summary: Youri Regeer was na afloop van de zege op Villarreal ( 1-2 ) tevreden over zijn nieuwe rol als leiderstype binnen het elftal en ging in op het akkefietje van afgelopen weekend in de businessclub van Ajax.

items.title: Mbappé and Vinícius lead Real Madrid resurgence as Monaco are hit for six
items.summary: The scars remain and there is much to be fixed still but this was a step towards reconciliation. It was something of a statement too, and not just in the goals scored but the reaction to them. Nine days after Xabi Alonso’s sacking, six after their captain said they had hit rock bottom with Copa del Rey elimination and three after the protest of a generation, white hankies and whistles greeting the players and even the president, there were songs and support at last as Real Madrid defeated Monaco 6-1. Kylian Mbappé, Franco Mastantuono and Jude Bellingham all scored, while there was an own goal from Monaco’s Thilo Kehrer. More significantly still, the man who had been at the centre of the storm made three of the six goals and smashed in a superb strike of his own. And if Vinícius Júnior, who had stood accused before the game and stood with the MVP trophy at the end of it, wasn’t entirely ready to share his moment with fans yet, his first goal here since October saw teammates come to his side. He then hugged his new coach, Álvaro Arbeloa, who claimed that was the embrace of the whole of Madrid. Continue reading...

items.title: Gabriel Jesus fulfils ‘dream’ at Inter as Arsenal celebrate qualification in style
items.summary: Mission accomplished for Arsenal. A seventh win out of seven ensured Mikel Arteta’s side will head straight into the last 16 of this competition as one of the two top seeds after Gabriel Jesus scored twice – including their 19th goal of the season from a corner – to see off last year’s beaten finalists. It means that as well as getting one back over an Inter team that they lost to 14 months ago, Arsenal have surpassed their longest winning streak at this level. While Manchester City’s surprise defeat in Norway in the earlier kick-off had removed any jeopardy about them progressing, this was more evidence of the ruthless streak Arsenal have developed under Arteta. The only blot on the copybook in a fourth successive away game in four different competitions was Petar Sucic’s equaliser in the first half after Jesus had given Arsenal an early lead, although this was all about the Brazil striker even after the substitute Viktor Gyökeres sealed the points late on with a classy finish. Continue reading...

items.title: Solanke leads Tottenham past 10-man Dortmund to offer relief for Frank
items.summary: It was a contender for shock result of the season. Nobody had given Tottenham any hope after the Premier League disaster here against West Ham on Saturday, one which came coated in vitriol for Thomas Frank. The fans had demanded his immediate removal as the manager, only for him to stagger on. The execution was stayed. But here were Borussia Dortmund, the Bundesliga’s second-placed team, who had lost only three games all season, to apply the final cut. Continue reading...

items.title: Jesus and Gyokeres give Arteta selection headache as Arsenal beat Inter
items.summary: Arsenal celebrated their qualification to the Champions League last 16 in style as they beat Inter Milan courtesy of Gabriel Jesus and Viktor Gyokeres.

items.title: 'Battered in Bodo' - is this more than just a blip for Man City?
items.summary: Manchester City suffered their very own "hell of a beating" moment in Norway on Tuesday - now Pep Guardiola and his players must turn around their fortunes in an increasingly trying January.
